scope: B9.2 predeclared quality / analyzability criteria. Fixed BEFORE acquisition; applied BLIND to scientific endpoints (never chosen after seeing p-values/effect sizes). Development-only, NO tag.

artifact_rejection.pipeline: predeclared automated rejection (e.g. amplitude/variance/flatline thresholds + standard EOG/EMG handling), fixed in this file before recording
artifact_rejection.applied: per trial, before any contract/statistical analysis; the specific thresholds are frozen here, not tuned on outcomes

analyzable_subject_definition.cell: subject x microblock x C x Y_design
analyzable_subject_definition.rule: a subject is ANALYZABLE iff, after artifact rejection, it retains both conditions with >= min_epochs_per_condition (=8, the B9.0/B3 default) AND its retained trials preserve within-(subject,microblock,Y_design) C-balance within bal_tol=0 (whole-microblock loss allowed; partial-cell imbalance is NOT rebalanced)
analyzable_subject_definition.attrition_semantics: dropped trials/subjects are a SUBSET of the pinned table (joined on trial_id). Balance-preserving loss -> REDUCED SUPPORT (INSUFFICIENT_LABELS_OR_SUPPORT if below threshold). Loss that induces C x Y_design imbalance or a prior shift P(Y_design|C) > prior_tol=0.05 -> CONTRACT_INVALID_OR_OUT_OF_ESTIMAND. Rejection is NEVER used to post-hoc rebalance.

support_threshold.note: n_eligible >= min_confirm_pairs = 20 is REQUIRED for any alert-level analysis; UNCHANGED. Target 24 reserves for attrition.

sampler_feasibility: the exact randomization null must be runnable (eligible complete pairs >= n_folds*2, fold prep non-degenerate); checked at the checkpoint WITHOUT computing any p-value
blinding: quality/analyzability decisions use ONLY logistics + support (adherence, attrition, artifact rate, per-cell counts, sampler feasibility, contract-invalid reasons) -- NEVER B9_ALERT / p-values / observed_T / T_z / injected-positive results
